back: A set of characters where each one has a unique binary code.
Characters are converted into binary so they can be processed by a computer.
A character set that uses 7 or 8 bits to represent characters.

front: How many bits are used in standard ASCII?
How many characters can 7-bit ASCII represent?
How many bits are used in extended ASCII?
How many characters can 8-bit extended ASCII represent?
What is a limitation of ASCII?
back: It does not contain enough characters for all languages and symbols.
A character set that can represent many more characters than ASCII.
To store and process many languages, symbols, and emojis.

front: What is one effect of Unicode using more bits per character?
